strengths: 15+ years of engineering experience with demonstrated expertise in high-scale distributed systems (Dunnhumby: million RPS microservices, real-time databases, low-latency infrastructure)
Current hands-on technical work with agentic frameworks and MCP infrastructure at re:cinq, directly engaging with core domain technologies
Strong software craftsmanship orientation (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) with active mentorship, teaching, and cross-platform technical versatility
concerns: Career trajectory is individual contributor → staff engineer with no evidence of leading or scaling teams; current role (Staff Engineer) is an IC position
Fintech infrastructure experience limited to 1.25 years at RIDE Capital (2020–2021); no background in credit systems, compliance, regulatory frameworks, or high-stakes financial operations
Agentic AI production experience is very recent (14 months); depth in multi-agent system governance, evaluation pipelines, observability, cost modeling, and failure modes not evident

key_factors.factor: Team leadership and organizational scaling experience
Current hands-on agentic framework technical engagement

response.justification: Szabo demonstrates strong backend engineering fundamentals and recent hands-on experience with agentic systems, but the resume reflects an individual-contributor technical trajectory rather than organizational leadership path. Critical gaps include no evidence of team scaling (CTO hard requirement of 10→30+ people), minimal fintech exposure (stated as meaningful advantage), and no experience operating in regulated or high-stakes commercial environments. His technical depth in distributed systems and current AI infrastructure work are relevant, but the organizational leadership, hiring, governance, and board-level communication experience essential for executive CTO leadership is not demonstrated.
